title: Homepage metadata renders a bogus offline-chain warning for non-offline partial data
reasoning: `buildDescription` assumes every `partial` result has at least one offline chain and emits `Partial — ${offlineChains} offline`. The OG data helper intentionally sets `partial=true` when pool decimal trust flags are missing even if `offlineChains` is empty, and the helper test pins that state. In that case the homepage metadata description becomes `Partial —  offline`, which is malformed and misdiagnoses the degraded condition as a chain outage.
reproduction: Mock `fetchHomepageOgData()` to return `{ partial: true, offlineChains: [], totalTvlUsd: null, totalVolume7dUsd: null, poolCount: 2, chains: ["Celo", "Monad"], healthBuckets: {} }`, call `generateMetadata()`, and inspect `description`.
recommendation: Only include the offline-chain phrase when `offlineChains.length > 0`; otherwise use a generic partial/degraded-data phrase such as `Partial data` before appending the remaining metrics.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included homepage route tests import `page-client` explicitly and do not execute `ui-dashboard/src/app/page.tsx`, `generateMetadata`, or `buildDescription`. The existing OG helper test covers the partial-with-empty-offlineChains producer state, but no route-level metadata test covers its consumer formatting.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a route-level test for `generateMetadata()` that mocks `fetchHomepageOgData()` with `partial: true` and `offlineChains: []`, then asserts the returned description does not contain `offline` and does contain a generic partial-data marker.
minimumFixScope: Update `buildDescription` in `ui-dashboard/src/app/page.tsx` and add a focused metadata formatting test.

history.note: Fixed by using generic 'Partial data' homepage metadata when partial data has no offline chains; added metadata formatting regression coverage.
